text:
Once upon a moonlit night in the tiny village of Everdale, there lived a fox named Flick. Flick was a crafty little fox who spent his days scamming the birds with delicate, mesmerizing feathers. He had an artful tail tufted with faux down, and a sly grin never failed to charm pigeons from their perches.

Flick fancied himself quite the romance novelist. Each evening, after feasting on berries stolen straight from Mrs. Hedgehog’s garden, he’d sit beside his secret window, dreaming up adventures. One evening, he had written himself into such a tale that he couldn't resist meddling.

Flick decided he wanted to save the lovely Princess Pecksniff from heartbreak. With the quick wit she knew so well, she might just be saved. Flick gathered his feather pen, inkful of midnight blue, and set out.

He’d sneak into the castle during the night, wearing a disguise: a cloak made from
